# vcorelib ([3.4.7](https://pypi.org/project/vcorelib/))

[![python](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/vcorelib.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/vcorelib/)
![Build Status](https://github.com/vkottler/vcorelib/workflows/Python%20Package/badge.svg)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/vkottler/vcorelib/branch/master/graphs/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://codecov.io/github/vkottler/vcorelib)
![PyPI - Status](https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/vcorelib)
![Dependents (via libraries.io)](https://img.shields.io/librariesio/dependents/pypi/vcorelib)

*A collection of core Python utilities.*

## Documentation

### Generated

* By [sphinx-apidoc](https://vkottler.github.io/python/sphinx/vcorelib)
(What's [`sphinx-apidoc`](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/man/sphinx-apidoc.html)?)
* By [pydoc](https://vkottler.github.io/python/pydoc/vcorelib.html)
(What's [`pydoc`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pydoc.html)?)

## Python Version Support

This package is tested with the following Python minor versions:

* [`python3.12`](https://docs.python.org/3.12/)
* [`python3.13`](https://docs.python.org/3.13/)

## Platform Support

This package is tested on the following platforms:

* `ubuntu-latest`
* `macos-latest`
* `windows-latest`

# Introduction

Core technologies shared between a number of other Python packages.

# Internal Dependency Graph

A coarse view of the internal structure and scale of
`vcorelib`'s source.
Generated using [pydeps](https://github.com/thebjorn/pydeps) (via
`mk python-deps`).

![vcorelib's Dependency Graph](im/pydeps.svg)

Provide data loaded from a file as a context so that it's written back
when the context ends.

documentation: Provide a loaded directory as a context.
A class implementing context-manager interfaces for the data arbiter.

modules.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter
documentation: A module aggregating all data-arbiter capabilities.

classes.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter.DataArbiter
documentation: A class aggregating all data-arbiter capabilities.

modules.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter.directory
documentation: A data-arbiter extension for working with directories.

classes.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter.directory.DataArbiterDirectories

methods.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter.directory.DataArbiterDirectories.encode_directory
documentation:
Encode data to a directory where every key becomes a file with the
provided extension. The encoding scheme is implied by the extension.

methods.name: vcorelib.io.arbiter.directory.DataArbiterDirectories.decode_directory
documentation:
Attempt to decode data files in a directory. Assigns data loaded from
each file to a key, returns whether or not any files failed to load
and the cumulative time that each file-load took.
A class adding interfaces for working with directories.

modules.name: vcorelib.io.archive
documentation: A module for extracting and creating arbitrary archives.

functions.name: vcorelib.io.archive.is_within_directory
documentation: Determine if a target path is within the provided directory.

documentation: Sanity check that all members will fall within the destination path.

functions.name: vcorelib.io.archive.extractall
documentation:
Attempt to extract an arbitrary archive to a destination. Return whether or
not this succeeded and how long it took.

functions.name: vcorelib.io.archive.make_archive
documentation:
Create an archive from a source directory, named after that directory,
and optionally moved to a destination other than the parent directory
for the source. The extension specifies the kind of archive to create.

Return the path to the created archive (if it was created) and how long
it took to create.

method returns True.

documentation: Update our internal tracking.

methods.name: vcorelib.paths.info_cache.FileInfoManager.poll_existing
documentation:
Poll all existing files. This is the only way to detect files that have
been deleted.

methods.name: vcorelib.paths.info_cache.FileInfoManager.poll_directory
documentation:
Poll an entire directory for changes. If files previously in this
directory were polled and are no longer present, they won't be polled
and thus won't trigger the callback. Use 'poll_existing' for that
behavior.

methods.name: vcorelib.paths.info_cache.FileInfoManager._call_callback
documentation:
Call the provided callback with the change event. Log information if
a logger is provided.

methods.name: vcorelib.paths.info_cache.FileInfoManager._poll_path
documentation: Get information about a file-system path.

methods.name: vcorelib.paths.info_cache.FileInfoManager.poll_file
documentation: Check if a file has changed and invoke the callback if so.
A class simplifying evaluation of changes to files on disk.

modules.name: vcorelib.paths.base
documentation: A module implementing common pathing utilities.

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.normalize
documentation: Normalize an input that could be a path into a path.

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.rel
documentation:
Attempt to make 'path' relative to base (which is the current-working
directory, if not provided).

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.set_exec_flags
documentation: Set the executable bits, but respect the 'read' bits.

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.stats
documentation: Get stats for a file on disk if it exists.

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.get_file_name
documentation:
From a path to a file, get the name of the file. Use 'maxsplit' to control
how many suffixes are considered part of the name or the extension.

functions.name: vcorelib.paths.base.get_file_ext
documentation:
From a path to a file, get the file's extension. Use 'maxsplit' to control
how many suffixes are considered part of the name or the extension.

modules.name: vcorelib.dict.env
documentation:
Utilities for working with environment-variable data contained inside various
data structures.

functions.name: vcorelib.dict.env.str_resolve_env_var
documentation:
Convert string data to a resolved environment variable if the string begins
with '$' and is a key in the environment with a non-empty value.

functions.name: vcorelib.dict.env.list_resolve_env_vars
documentation:
Recursively resolve list data that may contain strings that should be
treated as environment-variable substitutions. The data is updated
in-place.

functions.name: vcorelib.dict.env.dict_resolve_env_vars
documentation:
Recursively resolve dictionary data that may contain strings that should be
treated as environment-variable substitutions. The data is updated
in-place.

modules.name: vcorelib.dict
documentation: Dictionary manipulation utilities.

functions.name: vcorelib.dict.consume
documentation:
Attempt to obtain dictionary data via key, removing the data if it was
present.

documentation:

documentation: Ensure that dictionary data is only temporarily added.

functions.name: vcorelib.dict.merge_recursive
documentation:
Combine two dictionaries recursively, prefers dict_a in a conflict. For
values of the same key that are lists, the lists are combined. Otherwise
the resulting dictionary is cleanly merged.

documentation: Combine two dictionaries based on a provided merge strategy.

modules.name: vcorelib.task.manager

documentation: Register task dependencies while the event loop is running.

methods.name: vcorelib.task.manager.TaskManager.evaluate
documentation:
Iterate over task strings and provide a list of tasks that can be
executed (plus relevant data from matching the target) as well as
the set of tasks that can't be resolved.

methods.name: vcorelib.task.manager.TaskManager.prepare_execute
documentation:
Gather tasks that can and can't be executed to prepare a function that
can execute tasks and also return the task strings that wouldn't be
resolved.

methods.name: vcorelib.task.manager.TaskManager.execute
documentation:
Execute some set of provided tasks. Return the tasks that don't get
resolved.

A class for managing concurrent execution of tasks and also interfacing
them via names.

Register other tasks' output data to your input box. Return true

Register multiple dependencies. Return the number of dependencies

dependency resolver cannot propagate current-task substitutions to
its dependencies as they've already been explicitly registered.
